steps.body: **Kinetic energy** is energy of motion: **KE = ½mv²**. Faster objects and heavier objects carry more KE. Work done on an object changes its KE.

steps.prompt: A **2 kg** block moves at **3 m/s**. Drag the **KE** bar to its kinetic energy (use g = 9.8 m/s² only if needed — you won't here).
**Net work = 24 J** is done on the same **2 kg** block (starting from rest). Drag **ΔKE**.
hints: All 24 J of net work goes into kinetic energy → ΔKE = 24 J.

steps.prompt: Starting from rest, **24 J** of work is done on **m = 2 kg**. Drag the final **KE** bar (same as ΔKE here).

steps.title: Lesson complete!
steps.body: You connected work to kinetic energy and read KE from ½mv².
